content.text: The current cryptocurrency market shows a range of asset prices. Wrapped Bitcoin (WBTC) is trading at $80,488.74, while Wrapped Ethereum (WETH) is valued at $2,018.31. Solana (SOL) is priced at $126.42, and ai16z is at $0.1925.
A fix was implemented to resolve a missing moment rollup external issue in the-org build.
Core types were added to enable the generation of index.d.ts in the /dist directory, addressing errors related to missing types in imports.

A user reported being unable to get past the pnpm install and build process due to an issue with switching pnpm to version 9.15.7. Despite forcing the version switch, build commands failed due to missing pnpm CLI files. The issue affected multiple packages, including @elizaos/core and @elizaos/client-direct.

categories.title: Issues and Fixes in ElizaOS Plugins

content.text: A user reported an issue with the '@elizaos-plugins/client-twitter' plugin, which fails to process Twitter actions despite all other plugins working correctly. The error persists regardless of whether 'ragKnowledge' is enabled or disabled. Logs indicate issues with embedding settings and invalid message inputs. Additionally, there is a missing package error for '@elizaos-plugins/client-telegram'.

A fix was introduced for the '@elizaos-plugins/client-telegram' plugin by adding a 'serviceType' property. This change allows for multi-platform support, particularly for Ruby, enabling cross-platform testing. The fix consists of a single line of code defining 'static serviceType = TELEGRAM_SERVICE_NAME'.
A fix was implemented to address a missing moment rollup external in the ElizaOS build.
Core types were added to enable the generation of index.d.ts in the /dist directory, resolving missing type errors on imports.
A user reported being unable to get past 'pnpm install' and 'build' due to a missing pnpm CLI error. Despite forcing pnpm to switch to v9.15.7, the build process still failed.

An issue was raised regarding an outdated tutorial on building a Social AI Agent in 15 minutes. The embedded video and textual instructions no longer align with the current implementation, particularly regarding Twitter integration.
A user reported an 'Error processing tweet undefined' issue when using the Twitter plugin. Despite ensuring the fork was up to date, Twitter actions failed to process.
A fix was introduced for NEAR AI image generation, ensuring the correct API invocation and preventing fallback to OpenAI.
A minor fix was made to the Telegram plugin, adding a serviceType to support multi-platform functionality for Ruby.
Six contributors merged two pull requests, focusing on various improvements.
